Q1_BUSINESS_DESCRIPTION.question: Tell Us About Your Business — describe your business and your biggest retention challenge.
Q1_BUSINESS_DESCRIPTION.user_response: HelpDesk Pro is a B2B SaaS platform that helps mid-market companies manage customer support tickets across email, chat, and social media. We serve 500+ companies with 50-500 employees each, ranging from $2K-$15K MRR. Our biggest retention challenge is that customers seem to churn right around the 6-month mark — we see a 40% churn spike at month 6. We suspect it's because onboarding is weak (customers don't integrate with their existing tools), but we're not sure. We've been growing fast (200% YoY) but retention has flatlined.

Q2_DATA_UPLOAD_AND_CHURN_DEFINITION.question: What does churn mean in your data?

Q2_BUSINESS_TYPE_CUSTOMER_STAGE.question: Business Type, Customer & Stage
Q2_BUSINESS_TYPE_CUSTOMER_STAGE.stage_implications: High acquisition focus; retention problem likely due to onboarding speed not catching up with sales growth

Q3_REVENUE_MODEL_PRICING_FLEXIBILITY.question: Revenue Model & Pricing Flexibility
Q3_REVENUE_MODEL_PRICING_FLEXIBILITY.flexibility_notes: CEO wants to protect margins; can discount up to 20% for annual commitments, can extend trials from 14 to 30 days

Q4_PRODUCT_FEATURES_FLEXIBILITY.question: Product — Features & Flexibility
Q4_PRODUCT_FEATURES_FLEXIBILITY.natural_completion_point: Partially - support work is never finished, but new features plateau after 6 months

Q5_ACQUISITION_COMPETITION.question: Acquisition & Competition
top_acquisition_channels: Paid ads (Google, LinkedIn): 45% of new customers
Q5_ACQUISITION_COMPETITION.where_customers_go_after_churn: To a competitor (primarily Freshdesk for price, Zendesk for features)
Q5_ACQUISITION_COMPETITION.competitive_advantage: Ease of setup, lower cost than Zendesk, better UX than Freshdesk

Q6_OPERATIONAL_CAPACITY_CURRENT_EFFORTS.question: Operational Capacity & Current Efforts
retention_tactics_in_place: Onboarding flow / tutorials: Basic email drip, no in-app onboarding
Health scoring / at-risk alerts: Manual email check-ins at month 3
None: No CSM, no proactive outreach, no win-back campaigns
Q6_OPERATIONAL_CAPACITY_CURRENT_EFFORTS.capacity_constraints: 4-person team cannot scale CSM; engineering focused on feature parity

Q7_PRIORITY_GOAL_TIMELINE.question: Priority, Goal & Timeline
Q7_PRIORITY_GOAL_TIMELINE.customer_segment_priority: Newest customers (first 90 days) - high early churn
Q7_PRIORITY_GOAL_TIMELINE.risk_appetite: Medium - willing to try tactical changes, not overhaul

Q8_ANYTHING_ELSE_OPTIONAL.question: Anything Else? Constraints, past failures, internal debates, upcoming changes
Q8_ANYTHING_ELSE_OPTIONAL.user_response: We tried a win-back campaign 6 months ago (offered $100 credit) — got 12% reactivation but cost us $8K in credits. We also hired 2 salespeople 3 months ago who are now bringing in new customers fast, but onboarding hasn't scaled. CEO is pushing for upsell focus (expand revenue from existing customers) instead of reducing churn. We're also planning a major product launch (AI-powered ticket summarization) in 4 months.
past_failures: Win-back campaign: Low ROI (12% reactivation, $8K cost)
internal_debates: CEO wants upsell focus vs. retention focus
Engineering wants to build new features vs. improve onboarding
upcoming_changes: 2 new salespeople (3 months ago) - fast growth
Major product launch (AI ticket summarization) - 4 months out
edge_cases: Onboarding not scaling with sales growth
Product roadmap misaligned with churn problem

detected_patterns.adaptive_question: 35% of your customers leave within 90 days. Walk us through onboarding — is it self-serve, guided, or a mix? How many customers actually complete integration during trial?
Clear churn spike at month 6. Are there any product limits that kick in at 6 months (feature gates, trial extensions ending)? Or is this when annual commitments come up for renewal?

detected_patterns.metric: Paid ads customers churn at 42%, organic at 18%
detected_patterns.adaptive_question: Customers from paid ads churn 2.3× more than organic/referral. Are you setting expectations clearly in ads? Are they getting lower-touch onboarding?

data_richness.recommendation: High-quality input. System has strong directional clarity.

recommendation_confidence.note: Strong input context makes diagnosis more confident. Early churn + paid ads signal is clear.
